claim_or_signal: NVIDIA reported Q4 FY2026 revenue of $68.1B, up 73% year over year, and Data Center revenue of $62.3B, up 75% year over year.
memo_section: Executive Summary; AI Scaling Demand Chain
limitation: Revenue is a demand proxy, not a direct measure of GPU units, HBM allocation, or customer-level supply.

claim_or_signal: NVIDIA said full-year FY2026 Data Center revenue rose 68% to $193.7B, driven by accelerated computing and AI platform shifts.
limitation: Data Center includes compute, networking, systems, and software; it does not isolate HBM demand.

claim_or_signal: NVIDIA describes H200 as offering 141GB of HBM3e memory at 4.8TB/s, nearly double H100 capacity and 1.4x H100 memory bandwidth.
limitation: Vendor product page; use for technical specifications, not market demand forecasts.

claim_or_signal: NVIDIA lists GB200/GB300 NVL72 systems around large aggregate HBM3E capacity and bandwidth, showing that rack-scale AI systems are marketed around memory as well as compute.
memo_section: Why Memory Bandwidth Matters; AI Scaling Demand Chain
limitation: Exact system configuration may vary by generation and SKU; cite the specific page and avoid generalizing to all deployments.

claim_or_signal: AMD reported Q1 2026 revenue of $10.3B and said accelerating AI infrastructure demand made Data Center the primary driver of revenue and earnings growth.
limitation: Company commentary does not isolate Instinct GPU revenue, HBM supply, or end-customer allocation.

claim_or_signal: AMD says MI350 Series platforms integrate eight MI355X or MI350X GPU OAM modules with 2.3TB total HBM3E memory and 64TB/s peak theoretical aggregate memory bandwidth.
limitation: Peak theoretical specs; not a workload-level realized bandwidth measure.

claim_or_signal: Micron said HBM revenue reached new highs and that AI has increased memory demand while making memory a strategic asset in AI architectures.
limitation: Company framing; the remark is directional and does not disclose exact HBM revenue in the excerpt.

claim_or_signal: Micron said AI server demand remains strong and both AI and traditional server demand are constrained by lack of adequate DRAM and NAND supply.
limitation: This is broader than HBM; distinguish general DRAM/NAND tightness from HBM-specific bottlenecks.

claim_or_signal: Micron announced volume shipments of HBM4 36GB 12H in Q1 calendar 2026, designed for NVIDIA Vera Rubin, and said the HBM4 production ramp and shipments were underway.
limitation: Design-in language is important but does not reveal allocation size or yield.

claim_or_signal: Micron expects supply-demand conditions for DRAM and NAND to remain tight beyond calendar 2026 and cites cleanroom constraints, long construction lead times, higher HBM trade ratio, higher HBM growth rates, and declining bits-per-wafer growth as supply constraints.
limitation: This is Micron's market outlook; use as one vendor's view and cross-check with competitors and customers.

claim_or_signal: Micron said its Singapore advanced packaging facility for HBM is on track to contribute meaningfully to HBM supply in calendar 2027.
memo_section: Bottleneck Decomposition; What To Track Next
limitation: Implies future relief but not 2026 availability; contribution size is undisclosed.

claim_or_signal: SK hynix announced volume production of 12-layer HBM3E with 36GB capacity.
limitation: Older but useful as a production milestone; does not quantify current capacity or customer allocation.

source: SK hynix CES 2026 AI memory release
claim_or_signal: SK hynix showcased next-generation AI memory products at CES 2026, including HBM4 16Hi 48GB and SOCAMM2.
memo_section: HBM Supply Chain; What To Track Next
limitation: Showcase material is a roadmap signal, not proof of volume shipment or qualification.

claim_or_signal: Samsung said its 2026 Memory Business aims to address customer demand through timely HBM4 shipments and expanded sales of AI-related products such as DDR5, SOCAMM2, and GDDR7.
memo_section: HBM Supply Chain; Public Signal Framework
limitation: Company guidance; does not disclose customer qualification status or exact HBM capacity.

claim_or_signal: Samsung said its foundry business began shipments of 4nm HBM base-die products in Q4 2025.
memo_section: HBM Supply Chain; Advanced Packaging and Base Die
limitation: Base-die shipment is one layer of HBM supply; it does not prove full HBM stack volume or customer acceptance.

claim_or_signal: Samsung announced mass production of HBM4 and commercial shipments to customers.
limitation: Customer names, quantities, and qualification breadth are not disclosed in the release.

claim_or_signal: TSMC said Q1 2026 revenue increased 8.4% quarter over quarter, supported by strong demand for leading-edge process technologies; advanced technologies at 7nm and below accounted for 74% of wafer revenue.
memo_section: AI Scaling Demand Chain; Foundry Capacity
limitation: Leading-edge demand is a broad proxy; it includes more than AI accelerators.

claim_or_signal: TSMC said HPC represented 61% of Q1 2026 net revenue, while smartphone represented 26%.
memo_section: AI Scaling Demand Chain; Foundry Capacity
limitation: HPC is broader than AI accelerators; do not equate all HPC revenue with AI.

claim_or_signal: TSMC management said advanced packaging capacity is very tight and that it works with OSAT partners to increase capacity to support customers.
limitation: Transcript is edited by LSEG; avoid excessive quotation and cite as management commentary.

claim_or_signal: TSMC said CapEx in the next three years is expected to be significantly higher than the prior three years due to conviction in the AI megatrend.
memo_section: Capex Lead Time; What To Track Next
limitation: Capex is not all advanced packaging or AI; allocation details matter.

claim_or_signal: Meta reported 2025 capital expenditures of $72.22B and guided 2026 capex of $115B-$135B, with growth driven by investment supporting Meta Superintelligence Labs and the core business.
limitation: Capex includes data centers, servers, leases, networking, and other infrastructure; not a direct GPU or HBM measure.

claim_or_signal: Microsoft said Cloud and AI strength fueled Q3 FY2026 results, Azure and other cloud services revenue grew 40%, and its AI business surpassed a $37B annual revenue run rate.
limitation: AI revenue run rate is company-defined and does not map directly to capex, GPUs, or HBM.

claim_or_signal: Alphabet said Google Cloud revenue increased 48% to $17.7B in Q4 2025, led by GCP growth across enterprise AI infrastructure and AI solutions; it anticipated 2026 capex of $175B-$185B.
limitation: Capex includes technical infrastructure beyond AI; Google uses both TPUs and GPUs, so HBM exposure differs from GPU-only buyers.

claim_or_signal: Google said Cloud revenue grew 63% and exceeded $20B for the first time; it also said NVIDIA GPUs are part of its AI accelerator portfolio and that it expects to be among the first to offer Vera Rubin NVL72.
memo_section: AI Scaling Demand Chain; Public Signal Framework
limitation: CEO remarks are useful for direction but should be paired with financial filings for capex and revenue details.

claim_or_signal: Amazon said free cash flow declined primarily due to a $50.7B year-over-year increase in property and equipment purchases, primarily reflecting AI investments; it expected about $200B of 2026 capex across Amazon.
limitation: Amazon capex includes AWS, stores, logistics, chips, robotics, and other areas; not a pure AI infrastructure figure.

claim_or_signal: Amazon said Trainium2 was fully subscribed with 1.4M chips landed and that Trainium4 is expected to deliver 4x more memory bandwidth and 2x more high-memory-bandwidth capacity than Trainium3.
limitation: Trainium uses custom AWS silicon; useful for memory-intensity trend but not directly NVIDIA HBM demand.

claim_or_signal: TrendForce said HBM4 validation was expected in 2Q26 and that Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron were in final validation stages as NVIDIA Rubin became a key HBM4 demand catalyst.
limitation: Industry research summary; exact supplier shares and forecasts may be paid/proprietary.

claim_or_signal: TrendForce expected strong demand from cloud service providers and sovereign cloud to drive over 20% growth in AI server shipments by 2026.
limitation: Third-party forecast; methodology and full dataset may require paid access.

source: JEDEC home and HBM standards area
claim_or_signal: JEDEC identifies HBM as a main memory standard area and is the standards body for HBM-related specifications.
limitation: Standards access can require account/login; use public pages for definitions and cite technical product pages for implementation specs.

claim_or_signal: TSMC describes 3DFabric as its family of 3D silicon stacking and advanced packaging technologies, including CoWoS, InFO, and TSMC-SoIC, used to enable heterogeneous and homogeneous chip integration for high performance, compute density, energy efficiency, low latency, and integration.
limitation: Official technology page; useful for definitions and architecture, not capacity or allocation.

claim_or_signal: TSMC describes CoWoS as an advanced packaging technology used for high-performance computing applications and related HBM integration use cases.
limitation: Official technology page; use for definition, not as evidence of current tightness.

source: Microsoft AI infrastructure and Physical AI official blog
claim_or_signal: Microsoft said its AI infrastructure approach brings next-generation NVIDIA systems into Azure datacenters designed for power, cooling, networking, and rapid generational upgrades; it also described deploying liquid-cooled Grace Blackwell GPUs and powering on Vera Rubin NVL72 in labs.
limitation: Official company blog; useful for showing power/cooling as deployment design requirements, not proof of industry-wide constraint.

source: Microsoft Community-First AI Infrastructure official blog
claim_or_signal: Microsoft said AI infrastructure requires large-scale infrastructure development and that GPU chips powering AI workloads run at very high temperatures, requiring proper cooling; it also noted collaboration with utilities where water infrastructure constraints pose challenges.
limitation: Official company blog and policy framing; use as deployment-risk support, not a quantitative capacity estimate.
